data.title: Top Analyst Downgrades BigBear.ai Stock (BBAI) Rating but Lifts Price Target
data.description:
BigBear.ai (BBAI) stockâs rating was recently downgraded by Northland Securities analyst Michael Latimore to Hold from Buy. Despite the downgrade, the Top analyst raised the price target to $4 (19.8% upside potential from $2.5. This move reflects the analyst’s reaction over both the challenges BBAI is currently facing and future growth potential. BBAI stock dropped 20.5% in Fridayâs regular trading session.

Importantly, the move came after the company reported lower-than-expected fourth-quarter results. Further, BBAI provided a revenue forecast of $160 million to $180 million for 2025, which came below the analyst estimates of $193.9 million.

Key Reasons Behind the Downgrade

The analyst has cited three major reasons for the rating downgrade. These include a large revenue miss and a slight EBITDA miss in Q4, pointing to weak financial performance. Moreover, Latimore believes that since BBAI relies heavily on government contracts, unpredictable spending patterns could slow growth.

The downgrade also reflects the time required for the new CEO to implement strategic plans and drive meaningful and steady growth.

Factors That Could Boost BigBear.aiâs Performance

BigBear.ai is making several efforts to boost its performance and regain investor confidence. The company recently improved its financial position. In the fourth quarter, BBAI converted $58 million of debt into equity and reduced net debt to $27 million.

Moreover, BigBear.ai continues to secure major contracts, such as a $165 million production contract with the U.S. Army. These contracts not only drive revenue but also strengthen the companyâs foothold in the defense and intelligence sectors.

Lastly, by expanding into commercial sectors, BigBear.ai aims to tap higher-margin opportunities, reducing reliance on government contracts.

Is BBAI Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, BBAI stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based onÂ two Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. At $5.33, theÂ average BigBear.ai price targetÂ implies 59.58% upside potential.

See more BBAI analyst ratings.

Disclosure
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: U.S. Considers DeepSeek Ban on Government Devices Over Data Privacy Fears
data.description:
The U.S. government is considering banning the Chinese AI app DeepSeek from government devices due to national security concerns, according to the Wall Street Journal. This move adds to growing worries about foreign tech handling sensitive data.

Why DeepSeek Is a Concern

DeepSeek, launched in January 2025, quickly became popular for its advanced AI tools. But it also caught the eye of U.S. lawmakers over concerns about how it handles user data. The Chinese company says it stores data on servers in China, but officials believe it has not clearly explained how the data is used or who can access it.

The Trump administration is expected to introduce a rule blocking the download of DeepSeekâs chatbot on US government devices, sources said.

Officials are also weighing two other actions. They may ban the app from US app stores and restrict US cloud service providers from offering DeepSeekâs AI models. However, these discussions are still in the early stages, according to people familiar with the matter.

States and Other Countries Take Action against DeepSeek

The U.S. government has no full ban on DeepSeek, but some agencies, like the Navy and NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration), have blocked the app over security and privacy fears. New York has banned the app from state devices, warning that it could risk user privacy. Also, Texas and other states have taken steps to limit the appâs use.

The U.S. is not alone in its concerns regarding DeepSeek. Countries like Australia, Canada, and South Korea have already blocked the app from their government networks.

What Comes Next for DeepSeek

The proposed ban may not stop with government devices. U.S. officials are also considering removing DeepSeek from app stores and blocking its access to American cloud services.

If the U.S. moves forward with these restrictions, DeepSeek could face major setbacks. Being removed from app stores would limit user growth and reduce its presence in one of the worldâs largest tech markets.

Adding to its challenges, a recent NewsGuard audit found that DeepSeekâs chatbot had only 17% accuracy, with an 83% fail rate, worse than the 62% average of Western rivals like Microsoft-backed OpenAIâs ChatGPT (MSFT) and Googleâs Gemini (GOOGL). With growing scrutiny and potential bans, DeepSeekâs future in the U.S. remains uncertain.

data.title: Apple’s (AAPL) AI Struggles Deepen as Siri Upgrade Delayed Until 2026
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) has pushed back key AI upgrades for the Siri voice assistant, saying these enhancements will take âlonger than we thought.â They are now expected âin the coming year,â possibly extending into 2026. Meanwhile, rivals such as Alphabetâs (GOOGL) Google and Amazon (AMZN) continue to advance their AI capabilities.

These upgrades, initially unveiled in June 2024, aimed to make Siri smarter and more useful by allowing it to handle more complex tasks. This includes reading on-screen content, like spotting an address in a text and saving it to contacts. Also, it would let Siri search photos or manage tasks inside apps.

Appleâs AI Plans Face Delays

Despite Apple’s efforts to enhance Siri’s capabilities, many of the most awaited features remain unfinished.

Apple has not shared the exact cause for the delay, but reports say internal tests found bugs and slowdowns. These issues have raised concerns among top leaders. Fixing the issues may mean rebuilding parts of Siri, which could delay the timeline further.

At the same time, adding new AI features while keeping user data safe brings tough challenges for the company.

Rivals Race Ahead with Smarter AI Assistants

While Apple faces delays, Google and Amazon are moving fast with their AI initiatives. For instance, Googleâs Gemini AI now powers Samsungâs latest phones, giving users advanced voice help. Similarly, Amazon also launched a new version of Alexa that allows smoother, more human-like chats.

These moves put pressure on Apple to speed up its own AI work. Experts say Appleâs slow progress may hurt iPhone sales, as buyers want smarter tools. Still, the company plans to roll out Siriâs AI in more languages next month.

As Google and Amazon push ahead, Apple must act fast to keep up in the fierce AI race.

Is Apple a Buy, Sell, or Hold?Â 

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 33 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy, 11 Hold, and four Sell recommendations assigned in the last three months. TheÂ average AAPL price targetÂ of $250.95 implies 4.97% upside from current levels.

See more AAPL analyst ratings

Disclosure

data.title: Top Analyst Bangs the Drum on Super Micro Computer Stock
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) is a name that has courted plenty of controversy over the past year. The issue has centered on the reliability of its financials given the company delayed the filing of financial reports, which resulted in SMCI facing delisting from the NASDAQ. The disarray was such that Ernest Young resigned as its independent auditor, citing a lack of trust in management.

But the story has taken a turn. SMCI has finally submitted its overdue reports, regaining compliance with regulators â an event that Northland analyst Nehal Chokshi sees as a game-changer. According to Chokshi, the financials have been proven to be trustable with a âhigh degree of certainty.â

With that chapter closed, itâs time to shift the focus to what lies ahead for the company. To gain deeper insight, Chokshi, who ranks in the top 1% of Wall Street stock pros, recently engaged with SMCIâs investor relations team. So, what key takeaways did he uncover?

Well, for one, SMCI still has the same outlook for the March quarter, expecting results to range from a 12% decline to a 6% increase compared to the previous quarter. This is mainly because SMCIâs GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) customers are unlikely to be among the first to receive Nvidiaâs Blackwell GPUs.

Chokshi also notes that the Street expects Dellâs servers and networking business to remain flat sequentially in the April quarter, aligning with the midpoint of SMCIâs guide. This suggests that Nvidiaâs Blackwell shipments in the January quarter were primarily directed toward hyperscalers, which Dell and SMCI donât usually serve. The analyst also reminds investors that thereâs about an eight-week delay between Nvidia recognizing revenue and when server OEMs, like Dell and SMCI, reflect it in their earnings.

Looking ahead, Chokshi sees SMCIâs ambitious $40 billion revenue target for FY26 as achievable, though heâs maintaining a more conservative estimate of $30.9 billion â what he believes is the minimum the company will achieve. Meanwhile, industry checks suggest SMCI has a significant competitive edge in GenAI servers, largely due to its early adoption of Direct Liquid Cooling (DLC) technology. Additionally, its modular component design, rooted in its well-known Building Block Architecture, continues to be a key differentiator.

âAs such,â the 5-star analyst went on to say, âwe believe SMCI is well positioned to return to a share gain trajectory in GenAI servers minimally starting in F1Q26 (Sept Q), and as such we see SMCIâs $40B FY26 target (65% revenue growth relative to FY25 midpoint guidance) as reasonable.â

All told, Chokshi rates SMCI shares as an Outperform (i.e., Buy), while raising his price target from $57 to a joint Street-high of $70, implying the stock will gain 83% in the months ahead.

Yet, not everyone on Wall Street shares his enthusiasm. When it comes to ratings, the verdict is mixed: 4 Holds, 3 Buys, and 2 Sells leave SMCI with a cautious Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating. However, the average price target sits at $49.14, pointing to a still-solid 28.5% gain over the next year. (See SMCI stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanksâ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanksâ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: ‘Bears, Get Ready to Lose,’ Says Daniel Ives on Tesla Stock
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is currently receiving the thumbs down from car buyers worldwide, with sales plummeting in various regions as prospective consumers distance themselves from a brand synonymous with Elon Musk.

However, that fact appears inconsequential when considering the companyâs prospects. At least that is the opinion of Wedbush analyst and Tesla bull Daniel Ives.

âWhile the DOGE/Trump Musk iron clad partnership has created major brand worries for Tesla…we estimate less than 5% of Tesla sales globally are at risk from these issues despite the global draconian narrative for Musk,â the analyst said.

While Muskâs actions appear to be distancing the core Tesla consumer base, Ives points out that throughout the past decade, there have been several instances in its journey where negative sentiment and Street concerns have overshadowed the âunique disruptive global tech story.â From the tense production challenges in 2017/2018, to financial concerns, to the Musk/Twitter drama in 2022, and now once again, Tesla bulls are with their âback against the wall facing global negative sentimentâ due to Musk’s involvement with DOGE and the Trump administration. âThis is a gut check moment for the Tesla bulls (including ourselves) after this massive sell-off in Tesla shares with fears mounting,â Ives concedes.

For now, the bears âown the Tesla narrative,” and Muskâs political affiliations arenât doing him any favors. But Ives remains unfazed, believing that the upcoming catalysts will trump any negative sentiment. âThis is the start of the biggest innovation and technology cycle in Tesla’s history ahead over the next few years,â he confidently says, before running off a list of what will get Tesla back on track again.

First, before summer, there will be the launch of a new sub-$35k Model, which will âhelp drive pent-up EV consumer demand globallyâ â an interesting phrase considering how sales are tanking right now.

Then thereâs the rollout of unsupervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) in Austin this June â an event Ives sees as nothing short of transformational. In fact, he believes Teslaâs autonomous push alone could be worth $1 trillion, declaring it âthe biggest transformation to the auto industry in modern day historyâ and predicting Tesla will dominate the global autonomous market.

Ivesâ bullishness doesnât end there. In fact, the analyst thinks that Trump in the White House is âthe best thing that ever happened to Musk and Tesla.â Thatâs because it will establish a deregulatory environment with a federal autonomous roadmap âcentral to the Tesla golden strategic vision.â

Bottom line, Ives sees a massive opportunity ahead. So much so that heâs added TSLA to Wedbushâs Best Ideas List. Naturally, he rates the stock an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating and slaps a Street-high price target of $550 â a 109% upside from current levels. (To watch Ivesâ track record, click here)

Of course, opinions on Wall Street are split. While 13 analysts echo Ivesâ bullish stance, 11 remain on the sidelines with Hold ratings, and 11 are outright bearish. As a result, the consensus lands at Hold (i.e., Neutral). But even with that tempered outlook, thereâs still room for gains â at $347.96, the average price target suggests a 32% climb in the months ahead. (See Tesla stock forecast)

To find good ideas for stocks trading at attractive valuations, visit TipRanksâ Best Stocks to Buy, a tool that unites all of TipRanksâ equity insights.

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.

data.title: NVIDIA GTC 2025: AI Breakthroughs, Quantum Leap, and Big Market Moves
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA) has had an underwhelming start to the year, declining 16.08% year-to-date. However, the company is dusting off and ironing its suits for next weekâs grand technological occasion: the annual GPU Technology Conference (GTC) 2025, which will be held from March 17 to 21. The peak moment will occur when CEO Jensen Huang, the keynote speaker on March 18, is expected to unveil major AI and computing advancements.

With over 25,000 in-person attendees and 300,000 virtual viewers, the event is shaping up as the largest AI-focused conference of the year. Investors and industry experts will closely watch for product launches, strategic moves, and the companyâs evolving role in AI, cloud computing, and quantum technology.

What Is Expected at the GTC?

Thereâs great anticipation for significant reveals of the next-generation AI hardware, including potential updates to NVIDIAâs Hopper and Blackwell architectures, which power AI models and data centers. The company is also making a notable push into quantum computing, hosting its first-ever Quantum Day on March 20, signaling interest in hybrid AI-quantum solutions. Additionally, major advancements are expected in CUDA, AI supercomputing, and new cloud partnerships with Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN).

The conference will feature other key players from the tech industry, such as Google (GOOGL), AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), Tesla (TSLA), and OpenAI in attendance. AI startups and enterprise tech leaders will showcase innovations across industries, from self-driving cars to robotics and healthcare.

Although Nvidiaâs stock has been experiencing a rough start to 2025, showcasing new and advanced products or strategic shifts at GTC could drive NVDA shares higher. If Nvidia also proves it has a genuine foothold in quantum computing, it could solidify its leadership against rivals such as IBM (IBM) or Google. With the AI revolution accelerating, GTC 2025 is becoming a pivotal event for investors, researchers, and tech enthusiasts alike.

Is NVDA a Buy, or a Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, Nvidia is considered a Strong Buy, based on 42 analysts ratings. The average price target for NVDA stock is $177.41, suggesting a 57.43% upside potential.

See more NVDA analyst ratings
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: S&P 500 Faces One of Its Worst Slumps in History – Is Trump’s Trade Policy to Blame?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX), the worldâs leading index comprising the biggest 500 traded companies, is now lagging. In one of its worst weeks in decades, the index dropped 3.1%, erasing over $3 trillion in value since its February peak. Meanwhile, global markets have performed better, which might be signaling a shift in investor confidence. The U.S. share of global market capitalization has also fallen below 50%, marking a decline in dominance.

Several factors are driving this downturn. The U.S. economy shows signs of weakness, with declining home sales, rising unemployment claims, and slowing consumer spending. These concerns have created a solid infrastructure for investors’ hesitance, leading to market pullback. Nevertheless, the overall reaction to President Trump’s ongoing trade wars has had an adverse effect on the market and is considered a primary catalyst to the index’s recent slump.

Tariffs Uncertainty and Tech Stocks Decline

Trade policy uncertainty has added to the pressure. President Trumpâs tariffs, including a 25% tariff on Canadian and Mexican goods that has now been delayed to April, have clearly unsettled markets. Also, the ongoing trade tensions with China and threats of further tariffs on other nations have only worsened investor sentiment. With global trade facing potential disruptions, companies that rely on international markets could suffer, further weighing on stock performance.

amounted to roughly $854 billion. The technology sector, a major driver of the S&P 500, has been hit hard. Giants like Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) have lost hundreds of billions in market value, dragging the index down. For example, Nvidia has lost $35 in stock value since Jan. 23, which amounted to roughly $854 billion. Tesla has shed over $160 in stock value during the same time frame, accumulating over $500 billion in losses. A staggering numbers by all accounts.

Given its heavy tech weighting, the S&P 500 has struggled more than its global counterparts that are less reliant on this sector.

Investing Elsewhere?

The question now is whether this trend will continue. Historically, when the S&P 500 falls behind early in the year, it rarely recovers to outperform global markets. U.S. stocks may struggle if economic conditions and trade tensions donât improve. This raises an important question for American investors: Should they stick with U.S. stocks or start looking at global opportunities? If the current trends persist, diversification outside the U.S. could become an increasingly attractive option.

data.title: Chinese Investors Quietly Pour Millions into Elon Musk’s Companies
data.description:
Wealthy Chinese investors secretly funnel millions of dollars into private companies owned by Elon Musk, using complex financial structures that hide their identities. According to a report from the Financial Times, these investments are flowing into Muskâs private ventures, including SpaceX, Neuralink, and xAI.

To evade scrutiny, the funds are funneled through special-purpose vehicles (SPVs). While these structures are legal, they are often vague, making it difficult to identify the actual investors. This, of course, comes when the tension between the U.S. and China is at its highest, and Chinese capital in American high-tech sectors is increasingly unwelcome.

It’s a Bird, It’s a Plane, It’s the Chinese

Despite concerns about foreign influence, the primary motivation for these investments appears to be profit. With Chinaâs economy slowing, wealthy investors look for lucrative opportunities abroad. Many see Muskâs companies as a safe bet, especially since SpaceX continues to receive strong backing from the U.S. government and military.

One Chinese asset manager, Homaer Financial, has raised tens of millions of dollars from investors eager to buy into Muskâs empire. Some early backers saw massive returns, with one group reporting a 530% profit from a 2018 SpaceX investment.

However, direct Chinese investment in Muskâs ventures remains sensitive. In 2021, a Chinese companyâs attempt to publicly invest $50 million in SpaceX was blocked due to concerns over national security. Since then, investors have turned to offshore funds and indirect methods to get a piece of the action. With the future of U.S.-China relations uncertain, the use of these hidden financial channels may come under greater scrutiny. But for now, Chinaâs wealthy elite are quietly finding ways to ride the success of Elon Muskâs business empire.

What Is the Price Target for TSLA Stock?

With the companies mentioned in the article not traded on Wall Street, letâs see the consensus surrounding Muskâs publicly traded company, Tesla (TSLA). Based on 35 analystsâ ratings, Tesla is considered a Hold. The average price target for TSLA stock is $347.96, implying a 32.47% upside potential.

See more TSLA analyst ratings
data.author: Ran Melamed
